input.background: A laboratory kit states that the standard deviation of its results can be expected to vary with σ=1 unit. A lab technician takes four measurements using this kit.
input.question: Explain to a lay person the advantage of reporting the average of four measurements rather than using a single measurement.

output.answer: Assuming the measurements are unbiased, the average is more likely to reflect the true value of μ than is a single measurement.

measurement.description: The individual measurements taken by the lab technician using the kit.

sigma.description: The standard deviation of the kit's results as stated by the laboratory kit.

average_measurement.description: The average of the four measurements taken by the lab technician.
